metadata.title: States PCS: Gujarat GK Questions for GPSC Exams

questions.question: Who was appointed as the first Commerce Minister in independent India’s inaugural cabinet in 1947?
questions.explanation: C. H. Bhabha, a Parsi businessman, was the first Commerce Minister in independent India’s first cabinet (1947) and headed Works, Mines, and Power in the 1946 interim government. He led the Special Emergency Committee during communal riots.

questions.question: What was the capital city of Mularaja, the Chaulukya king of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Mularaja (r. 1175–1178 CE) was a Chaulukya king of Gujarat, ruling from Anahilapataka (Patan). His mother Naiki Devi was regent. The dynasty repulsed a Ghurid invasion in 1178 CE. The Paramara king Vindhyavarman contested Malwa during his reign.

questions.question: Dingal, an ancient Indo-Aryan poetic language known for its war poetry and unique poetic forms, is primarily associated with which community?
questions.explanation: Dingal, also known as Old Western Rajasthani, is an ancient Indo-Aryan poetic language used in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Kutch, Malwa, and Sindh. It is linked to Charan literature, war poetry, and is an ancestor of Marwari and Gujarati. Dingal preserves archaic vocabulary and has unique poetic forms like Dingal Gīt. Several historical Dingal dictionaries exist, authored by scholars from Marwar and Jaisalmer.

questions.question: What percentage of seats does the state of Gujarat reserve for Scheduled Tribes?
questions.explanation: Gujarat reserves 27% seats for OBC, 7.5% for Scheduled Castes, and 15% for Scheduled Tribes, totaling 49.5%, capped at 50% by the Supreme Court. The 1981 SEBC reservation, mainly for Koli caste, led to riots and political shifts. Patidars constitute 12.3% of Gujarat’s population.

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled the Sindh Sultanate from 1554 until its annexation by Mughal emperor Akbar in 1593?
questions.explanation: The Tarkhan dynasty ruled the Sindh Sultanate from 1554 to 1593, founded by General Mirza Isa Beg after the Arghun dynasty. Mughal emperor Akbar annexed Sindh in 1593, ending Tarkhan rule; Arghun rulers are buried at Makli Necropolis.

questions.question: Who founded the Baroda State in 1721?
questions.explanation: Baroda State, founded in 1721 by the Gaekwad dynasty, was a Maratha princely state in present-day Gujarat with Vadodara as its capital. It had four prants: Kadi, Baroda, Navsari, and Amreli. The Gaekwads acknowledged British suzerainty post-1805, retaining internal autonomy. The state established the Bank of Baroda (1908) and Baroda Legislative Assembly. It owned the Gaekwar’s Baroda State Railway ‘s first narrow-gauge railway (1862). The royal succession followed male primogeniture. The Most Noble Order of the Rising Sun was instituted by Sayajirao Gaekwad III. Baroda merged into India in 1949.

questions.question: Who was the personal secretary of Mahatma Gandhi, as mentioned in the biography ‘Agnikundma Ugelu Gulab’ authored by Narayan Desai?
questions.explanation: Narayan Desai, son of Mahadev Desai, authored the biography ‘Agnikundma Ugelu Gulab’ about Mahadevbhai Desai, Mahatma Gandhi’s personal secretary.

questions.question: On the banks of which river is the Sidhpur Camel Festival held annually in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: The Sidhpur Camel Festival in Gujarat, held annually on Sabarmati River banks during Kartik Purnima, features camel trading, cultural performances, and local festivities.

questions.question: In which district of Gujarat is the Ratanmahal Sloth Bear Sanctuary located?
questions.explanation: Ratanmahal Sloth Bear Sanctuary, established in 1982 in Dahod district, Gujarat, protects sloth bears and other wildlife, providing a dedicated habitat for their conservation.

questions.question: In which city did the Dutch East India Company establish a trading post in Gujarat in 1616?
questions.explanation: The Dutch East India Company established a trading post in Surat, Gujarat, in 1616, marking the start of Dutch trade in the region.
